title: [LowerToHW] Fix reg init via verbatim ops
body:
Work around a bug in Verilog emission with the disallowLocalVariables
option where temporaries are spilled to wires which violates the
ordering of the initial block used for register randomization.  This
manifests as X's showing up in registers after initialization.  This
avoids this entirely by using explicit registers and verbatim operations
using those registers.

Fixes #2310.

Signed-off-by: Schuyler Eldridge <schuyler.eldridge@sifive.com>

For the example FIRRTL circuit shown in #2310:

```verilog
circuit Foo:
  module Foo:
    input clock: Clock
    input d: UInt<1>
    output q: UInt<1>

    reg r: UInt<1>, clock

    r <= d
    q <= r
```

This PR produces, with or without `-lowering-options=disallowLocalVariables`:

```verilog
module Foo(	// Bar.fir:2:10
  input  clock, d,
  output q);

  reg r;	// Bar.fir:7:5

  `ifndef SYNTHESIS	// Bar.fir:7:5
    `ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT	// Bar.fir:7:5
      reg [31:0] _RANDOM;	// Bar.fir:7:5

    `endif
    initial begin	// Bar.fir:7:5
      `INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_	// Bar.fir:7:5
      `ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT	// Bar.fir:7:5
        _RANDOM = `RANDOM;	// Bar.fir:7:5
        r = _RANDOM[0];	// Bar.fir:7:5
      `endif
    end // initial
  `endif
  always @(posedge clock)	// Bar.fir:9:7
    r <= d;	// Bar.fir:9:7
  assign q = r;	// Bar.fir:2:10
endmodule
```
